name: Maintaining
tagline: Hold your physique while recovering for the next phase.
overview: Maintenance sustains your current physique while preserving muscle and strength with balanced nutrition and training.
It is often overlooked but critical for long-term success — a stable base between cutting and bulking.
Recommended duration is about 3 months between bulk and cut blocks so metabolism, habits, and recovery can settle.
nutritionGuidelines: Match calories to TDEE so weight stays relatively stable.
Carbohydrates: 3–5g per kg, adjusted to activity.
Fats: 0.8–1g per kg from balanced sources.
Aim for 3–4 balanced meals with consistency over perfection.

dailyRoutine.label: Breakfast
dailyRoutine.detail: Balanced meal with protein, carbs, and fats.

dailyRoutine.label: Lunch
dailyRoutine.detail: Protein-forward plate with vegetables and carbs.

dailyRoutine.label: Dinner
dailyRoutine.detail: Final balanced meal; keep calories near TDEE.

name: Cutting
tagline: Lose fat while protecting hard-earned muscle.
overview: Cutting uses a controlled calorie deficit to reduce body fat while prioritizing protein and training performance.
Progress comes primarily from fuel — without a deficit, fat loss stalls.
Keep the deficit moderate so strength and recovery stay workable.
nutritionGuidelines: Eat below TDEE with a sustainable deficit (often ~300–500 kcal).
Protein: keep high (around 2.0–2.4g per kg) to protect muscle.
Carbs: lower than maintenance but timed around training when helpful.
Fats: enough for hormones — do not crash dietary fat to zero.
Track weight weekly trends, measurements, and photos — not day-to-day noise.

dailyRoutine.label: Wake + weigh-in
dailyRoutine.detail: Same conditions each morning for trend data.

dailyRoutine.label: High-protein breakfast
dailyRoutine.detail: Start protein early to hit daily targets.

dailyRoutine.label: Train
dailyRoutine.detail: Protect performance; fuel around the session.

dailyRoutine.label: Dinner
dailyRoutine.detail: Finish remaining macros without binge swings.

dailyRoutine.label: Sleep
dailyRoutine.detail: Recovery is non-negotiable in a deficit.

name: Bulking
tagline: Eat in a surplus so growth is actually possible.
overview: Bulking requires a calorie surplus — without surplus, meaningful muscle growth is extremely limited for natural trainees.
Pair progressive training with enough food, protein, and sleep.
Keep the surplus controlled to grow without unnecessary fat gain.
nutritionGuidelines: Carbs: higher to support training performance and recovery.
Fats: fill remaining calories with quality sources.
If scale weight is flat for 2+ weeks, raise calories — training alone cannot force growth in a deficit.

dailyRoutine.label: Wake + breakfast
dailyRoutine.detail: Large protein + carb meal to start surplus.

dailyRoutine.label: Snack
dailyRoutine.detail: Calories between meals if appetite is low.

dailyRoutine.label: Train hard
dailyRoutine.detail: Progressive overload; see Bro Training for methods.
